incorrects.description: Student set wrong container count on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. Listen to what I asked for: 3 groups of 4. How many groups?

incorrects.description: Student set wrong container count on second attempt

params.description: The phrase '3 groups' highlights in the instruction area.

steps.text: Look at the instruction: 3 groups of 4. The first number tells you how many bags. How many bags do you need?

incorrects.description: Student set wrong container count three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: 3 bags appear on screen, demonstrated by the system.

steps.text: Let me show you. The problem says 3 groups of 4. Groups means bags. The first number, 3, tells me how many bags. So I need 3 bags. That's what groups means in multiplication.

incorrects.description: Student set wrong items per container on first attempt

steps.text: Almost. The instruction says 3 groups of 4. How many in each group?

incorrects.description: Student set wrong items per container on second attempt

params.description: The phrase 'of 4' highlights in the instruction area.

steps.text: Look closely: 3 groups of 4. The second number tells you how many items go in each bag. How many items in each?

incorrects.description: Student set wrong items per container three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: 4 items appear in each of the 3 bags, demonstrated by the system.

steps.text: Here's how it works. The instruction says 3 groups of 4. The second number, 4, tells me how many items go in each bag. So I put 4 items in each of the 3 bags. That builds 3 groups of 4. The number after 'of' tells you what's in each group.
